{{nihongo|'''Sengenyama Kofun'''|浅間山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Kuraganomachi neighborhood the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216009|title=浅間山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the second largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]]), and third in the Kantō region after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] and the [[Funazukayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Ishioka, Ibaraki|Ishioka, Ibaraki]]). It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano kofun cluster.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengenyama tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a plain to the southeast of the city center of Takasaki on the left bank of the Karasu River near the confluence of the Kabura River. The tumulus has a total length of 170.5 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], local inhabitants dug into the tumulus searching for the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], but discovered only vermillion painted fragments of stone and clay, and no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. The chamber itself may have been a pit-shaped clay-lined hole.
The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat, with the inner moat having a width of 20 to 30 meters and the outer moat having a width of 56 to 65 meters.
Within the same Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Ōtsurumaki Kofun]] (123 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
;Total length: 171.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 66.3 meters long x 74.8 meters wide x 5.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 105 meter diameter x 14.1 meters high, 3-tiers
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kuragano Station|Kuragano Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Takasaki Line|Takasaki Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki)]][[en:Sengenyama Kofun]][[ja:浅間山古墳 (高崎市)]][[zh:淺間山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki)]][[en:Sengenyama Kofun]][[ja:浅間山古墳 (高崎市)]][[zh:淺間山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki)]][[en:Sengenyama Kofun]][[ja:浅間山古墳 (高崎市)]][[zh:淺間山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11557462]] Wikidata name: Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in the Kantō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sengenyama Kofun | native_name =浅間山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki) zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengenyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengenyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.298947|N|139.033961|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Sengenyama Kofun'''|浅間山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Kuraganomachi neighborhood the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216009|title=浅間山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the second largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]]), and third in the Kantō region after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] and the [[Funazukayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Ishioka, Ibaraki|Ishioka, Ibaraki]]). It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano kofun cluster.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengenyama tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a plain to the southeast of the city center of Takasaki on the left bank of the Karasu River near the confluence of the Kabura River. The tumulus has a total length of 170.5 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], local inhabitants dug into the tumulus searching for the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], but discovered only vermillion painted fragments of stone and clay, and no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. The chamber itself may have been a pit-shaped clay-lined hole.
The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat, with the inner moat having a width of 20 to 30 meters and the outer moat having a width of 56 to 65 meters.
Within the same Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Ōtsurumaki Kofun]] (123 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
;Total length: 171.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 66.3 meters long x 74.8 meters wide x 5.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 105 meter diameter x 14.1 meters high, 3-tiers
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kuragano Station|Kuragano Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Takasaki Line|Takasaki Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{nihongo|'''Sengenyama Kofun'''|浅間山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Kuraganomachi neighborhood the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216009|title=浅間山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the second largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ōta}}), and third in the Kantō region after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] and the [[Funazukayama Kofun]] ({{ill|Ishioka, Ibaraki|en|Ishioka, Ibaraki|WD=Q819658|lt=Ishioka, Ibaraki}}). It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano kofun cluster.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengenyama tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a plain to the southeast of the city center of Takasaki on the left bank of the Karasu River near the confluence of the Kabura River. The tumulus has a total length of 170.5 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, local inhabitants dug into the tumulus searching for the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], but discovered only vermillion painted fragments of stone and clay, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. The chamber itself may have been a pit-shaped clay-lined hole.
The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat, with the inner moat having a width of 20 to 30 meters and the outer moat having a width of 56 to 65 meters.
Within the same Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Ōtsurumaki Kofun]] (123 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
;Total length: 171.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 66.3 meters long x 74.8 meters wide x 5.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 105 meter diameter x 14.1 meters high, 3-tiers
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}

It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano kofun cluster.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengenyama tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a plain to the southeast of the city center of Takasaki on the left bank of the Karasu River near the confluence of the Kabura River. The tumulus has a total length of 170.5 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, local inhabitants dug into the tumulus searching for the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], but discovered only vermillion painted fragments of stone and clay, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. The chamber itself may have been a pit-shaped clay-lined hole.
The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat, with the inner moat having a width of 20 to 30 meters and the outer moat having a width of 56 to 65 meters.
Within the same Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Ōtsurumaki Kofun]] (123 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
;Total length: 171.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 66.3 meters long x 74.8 meters wide x 5.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 105 meter diameter x 14.1 meters high, 3-tiers
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, local inhabitants dug into the tumulus searching for the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], but discovered only vermillion painted fragments of stone and clay, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. The chamber itself may have been a pit-shaped clay-lined hole.
The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat, with the inner moat having a width of 20 to 30 meters and the outer moat having a width of 56 to 65 meters.
Within the same Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Ōtsurumaki Kofun]] (123 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
;Total length: 171.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 66.3 meters long x 74.8 meters wide x 5.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 105 meter diameter x 14.1 meters high, 3-tiers
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki)]][[en:Sengenyama Kofun]][[ja:浅間山古墳 (高崎市)]][[zh:淺間山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki)]][[en:Sengenyama Kofun]][[ja:浅間山古墳 (高崎市)]][[zh:淺間山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki)]][[en:Sengenyama Kofun]][[ja:浅間山古墳 (高崎市)]][[zh:淺間山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in the Kantō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sengenyama Kofun | native_name =浅間山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki) zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengenyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengenyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.298947|N|139.033961|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki, Gunma]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Sengenyama Kofun'''|浅間山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Kuraganomachi neighborhood the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216009|title=浅間山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the second largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ōta|1=Ōta, Gunma}}), and third in the Kantō region after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] and the [[Funazukayama Kofun]] ({{ill|Ishioka, Ibaraki|en|Ishioka, Ibaraki|WD=Q819658|lt=Ishioka, Ibaraki|1=Ishioka, Ibaraki}}). It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano kofun cluster.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengenyama tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a plain to the southeast of the city center of Takasaki on the left bank of the Karasu River near the confluence of the Kabura River. The tumulus has a total length of 170.5 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, local inhabitants dug into the tumulus searching for the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], but discovered only vermillion painted fragments of stone and clay, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. The chamber itself may have been a pit-shaped clay-lined hole.
The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat, with the inner moat having a width of 20 to 30 meters and the outer moat having a width of 56 to 65 meters.
Within the same Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Ōtsurumaki Kofun]] (123 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
;Total length: 171.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 66.3 meters long x 74.8 meters wide x 5.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 105 meter diameter x 14.1 meters high, 3-tiers
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station|1=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line|1=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki)]][[en:Sengenyama Kofun]][[ja:浅間山古墳 (高崎市)]][[zh:淺間山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki)]][[en:Sengenyama Kofun]][[ja:浅間山古墳 (高崎市)]][[zh:淺間山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki)]][[en:Sengenyama Kofun]][[ja:浅間山古墳 (高崎市)]][[zh:淺間山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in the Kantō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sengenyama Kofun | native_name =浅間山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sengenyama-kofun (Takasaki) zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sengenyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sengenyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.298947|N|139.033961|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki, Gunma]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Sengenyama Kofun'''|浅間山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Kuraganomachi neighborhood the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216009|title=浅間山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the second largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ōta|1=Ōta, Gunma}}), and third in the Kantō region after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] and the [[Funazukayama Kofun]] ({{ill|Ishioka, Ibaraki|en|Ishioka, Ibaraki|WD=Q819658|lt=Ishioka, Ibaraki|1=Ishioka, Ibaraki}}). It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano kofun cluster.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sengenyama tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a plain to the southeast of the city center of Takasaki on the left bank of the Karasu River near the confluence of the Kabura River. The tumulus has a total length of 170.5 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, local inhabitants dug into the tumulus searching for the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], but discovered only vermillion painted fragments of stone and clay, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. The chamber itself may have been a pit-shaped clay-lined hole.
The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat, with the inner moat having a width of 20 to 30 meters and the outer moat having a width of 56 to 65 meters.
Within the same Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Ōtsurumaki Kofun]] (123 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
;Total length: 171.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 66.3 meters long x 74.8 meters wide x 5.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 105 meter diameter x 14.1 meters high, 3-tiers
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station|1=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line|1=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
